# terrible_cisco_intersight_intersight_macsec_policy (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name assigned to the MACsec Policy. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `cipher_suite` (String) Cipher suite to be used for MACsec encryption.
- `confidentiality_offset` (String) The MACsec confidentiality offset specifies the number of bytes starting from the frame header. MACsec encrypts only the bytes after the offset in a frame.
- `configure_fallback_keychain` (Boolean) Enable configuration of fallback keychain.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The user-defined description for the MACsec Policy. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).
- `eapol_ethertype` (String) Ethertype to use in EAPoL frames for MKA PDUs. The range is between 0x600 - 0xffff.
- `eapol_mac_address` (String) MAC address to use in extensible authentication protocol over LAN (EAPoL) for MKA PDUs. EAPol MAC address should not be equal to all-zero (0000.0000.0000).
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `fallback_keychain_name` (String) Fallback keychain name. Required when `configure_fallback_keychain` is `true`.
- `fallback_keys` (String) List of security keys for the fallback keychain.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `include_icv_indicator` (Boolean) Configures inclusion of the optional integrity check value (ICV) indicator. Part of the transmitted MACsec key agreement protocol data unit (PDU).
- `key_server_priority` (Number) Key server is selected by comparing priority values during MKA message exchange. Valid values range from 0 to 255. Lower value means higher chance of being selected as key server.
- `organization` (String) The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Profiles, Policies, and Pools that are created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `primary_keychain_name` (String) Primary keychain name for managing the default set of security keys.
- `primary_keys` (String) List of security keys for the primary keychain.
- `replay_window_size` (Number) Defines the size of the replay protection window. Determines the number of packets that can be received out of order without being considered replay attacks. Valid range is from 0 to 596000000.
- `sak_expiry_time` (Number) Time in seconds to force secure association key (SAK) rekey. Valid range is from 60 to 2592000 seconds. When set to 0 or not configured, SAK rekey interval is determined based on interface speed.
- `security_policy` (String) The security policy specifies the level of MACsec enforcement on network traffic. `should-secure` allows unencrypted traffic until MKA session is secured. `must-secure` only allows MACsec encrypted traffic.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) If `present`, will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If `absent`, will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `api_response` (String) The API response output returned by the specified resource.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
